clinical_coherence.notes: 22M Hispanic with anxiety, depression, childhood sexual abuse, and IBS presenting with left leg weakness and PNES. Male FND patient adds demographic diversity. Positive Hoover sign, give-way weakness, midline splitting present. Video-EEG captures events with no ictal correlate. Psychosocial stressor (occupational stress) present.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG normal with captured events showing no correlate. MRI normal. Labs normal. All tool outputs concordant with FND diagnosis and ground truth.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD F44.4 correct. Well-structured differential. Critical and contraindicated actions clinically appropriate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate. Clear positive signs and typical demographics despite being male (FND can occur in males, especially with trauma history).

demographic_plausibility.notes: 22M Hispanic with childhood sexual abuse and anxiety/depression is realistic for FND. While FND is more common in females, males with trauma history are well-represented in the literature. Medications appropriate.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case showing FND in a male patient, which challenges the gender stereotype. However, the clinical pattern is very similar to S01-S03, limiting incremental educational value within the case set.

strengths: Demographic diversity with male Hispanic patient
Demonstrates FND occurs in males with appropriate risk factors
Consistent clinical presentation with positive functional signs
